title: daucus carota subsp. bocconei
intro: Daucus carota subsp. bocconei, also known as the wild carrot or Parma violet, is a subspecies of the carrot plant that is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. bocconei include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural antihistamine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
